name: Wolfe et al. Real Essay Comparator Extract
population_or_context: Simulated student essays generated for automated-scoring data augmentation.
sample_size: 96 real student essays and 288 simulated essays generated across three prompt strategies.
methods: Extracted the same-source real essay comparator and prompt-specific score/realism comparison results.
education_context: Student essays scored with a four-point rubric.
response_origin: LLM-generated simulated essays are derived from real student essays and compared with the original real essays.
label_source: Original essays had professional scores; expert raters scored simulated essays and judged whether essays were simulated.
generation_model: Large language model; the public preprint text reviewed here does not name a specific model version.
generation_method: Prompt-based simulated essay generation from real essays.
prompt_strategy: 25 Examples, Predict Next, and Sentence prompting strategies are compared.
evaluation_method: Expert scoring, score-distribution comparison, exact agreement, quadratic weighted kappa, and simulation-detection judgments.
scorer_use: Data augmentation calibration for automated scoring, not direct scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: The original real student essays provide source essays, score distribution, and professional-score comparator.
reported_metrics: Table 2 reports average score, agreement with original student score, quadratic weighted kappa, and simulation-detection accuracy by prompt strategy.
limitations: The real comparator is the source essay used to condition generation, not an independent held-out real response pool.
Score agreement, average-score alignment, and human realism detection do not point to one uniformly best synthetic essay method.
